      try {
        var key = 'speakeasy-api-selected-language';
        var lang = localStorage.getItem(key);
        if (!lang || lang === 'null' || lang === 'undefined' || lang === '') {
          lang = 'typescript';
          localStorage.setItem(key, lang);
          console.log('Setting language to', lang);
        }
        document.documentElement.dataset.selectedLanguage = lang;
        window.API_SELECTED_LANGUAGE = lang;
        console.log('Setting language to', lang);
      } catch (e) {}
    })();

slot="description"><p class="text-secondary-foreground/93">The <code class="relative mx-1 bg-background !text-[0.765em] ring-1 ring-offset-1 ring-offset-background ring-border font-mono after:hidden before:hidden inline-flex items-center justify-center gap-2 rounded text-xs font-semibold text-foreground px-1">presence_penalty</code> determines how much the model penalizes the repetition of words or phrases. A higher presence penalty encourages the model to use a wider variety of words and phrases, making the output more diverse and creative.</p></article></div></div></div></div></div></div></div></div><div data-slot="connecting-cell" data-bottom-connection="none" data-top-connection="none" data-right-connection="none" slot="description"><p class="text-secondary-foreground/93">Specify the format that the model must output. By default it will use <code class="relative mx-1 bg-background !text-[0.765em] ring-1 ring-offset-1 ring-offset-background ring-border font-mono after:hidden before:hidden inline-flex items-center justify-center gap-2 rounded text-xs font-semibold text-foreground px-1">\{ &quot;type&quot;: &quot;text&quot; \}</code>. Setting to <code class="relative mx-1 bg-background !text-[0.765em] ring-1 ring-offset-1 ring-offset-background ring-border font-mono after:hidden before:hidden inline-flex items-center justify-center gap-2 rounded text-xs font-semibold text-foreground px-1">\{ &quot;type&quot;: &quot;json_object&quot; \}</code> enables JSON mode, which guarantees the message the model generates is in JSON. When using JSON mode you MUST also instruct the model to produce JSON yourself with a system or a user message. Setting to <code class="relative mx-1 bg-background !text-[0.765em] ring-1 ring-offset-1 ring-offset-background ring-border font-mono after:hidden before:hidden inline-flex items-center justify-center gap-2 rounded text-xs font-semibold text-foreground px-1">\{ &quot;type&quot;: &quot;json_schema&quot; \}</code> enables JSON schema mode, which guarantees the message the model generates is in JSON and follows the schema you provide.</p></article></div></div></div></div></div></div></div></div><div data-slot="connecting-cell" data-bottom-connection="none" data-top-connection="none" prose-code:text-[0.875em] prose-code:font-mono prose-sm text-secondary-foreground/70" slot="description"><p class="text-secondary-foreground/93">Whether to stream back partial progress. If set, tokens will be sent as data-only server-side events as they become available, with the stream terminated by a data: [DONE] message. Otherwise, the server will hold the request open until the timeout or until completion, with the response containing the full result as JSON.</p></article></div></div></div></div></div></div></div></div><div data-slot="connecting-cell" data-bottom-connection="none" data-top-connection="none" data-right-connection="none" class="group prose-h6:mb-[0.25em] prose-hr:border-secondary-foreground [&amp;_details]:text-foreground [&amp;_details&gt;summary_+_*]:mt-6 [&amp;_details_summary]:cursor-default prose-code:text-[0.875em] prose-code:font-mono prose-sm text-secondary-foreground/70" slot="description"><p class="text-secondary-foreground/93">What sampling temperature to use, we recommend between 0.0 and 0.7. Higher values like 0.7 will make the output more random, while lower values like 0.2 will make it more focused and deterministic. We generally recommend altering this or <code class="relative mx-1 bg-background !text-[0.765em] ring-1 ring-offset-1 ring-offset-background ring-border font-mono after:hidden before:hidden inline-flex items-center justify-center gap-2 rounded text-xs font-semibold text-foreground px-1">top_p</code> but not both. The default value varies depending on the model you are targeting. Call the <code class="relative mx-1 bg-background !text-[0.765em] ring-1 ring-offset-1 ring-offset-background ring-border font-mono after:hidden before:hidden inline-flex items-center justify-center gap-2 rounded text-xs font-semibold text-foreground px-1">/models</code> endpoint to retrieve the appropriate value.</p></article></div></div></div></div></div></div></div></div><div prose-blockquote:border-secondary-foreground prose-blockquote:text-secondary-foreground prose-headings:first:mt-0 prose-h1:mt-[1.6em] prose-h1:mb-[0.6em] prose-h2:mt-[1.4em] prose-h2:mb-[0.5em] [&amp;_details]:text-foreground [&amp;_details&gt;summary_+_*]:mt-6 [&amp;_details_summary]:cursor-default prose-code:text-[0.875em] prose-code:font-mono prose-sm text-secondary-foreground/70" slot="description"><p class="text-secondary-foreground/93">Controls which (if any) tool is called by the model. <code class="relative mx-1 bg-background !text-[0.765em] ring-1 ring-offset-1 ring-offset-background ring-border font-mono after:hidden before:hidden inline-flex items-center justify-center gap-2 rounded text-xs font-semibold text-foreground px-1">none</code> means the model will not call any tool and instead generates a message. <code class="relative mx-1 bg-background !text-[0.765em] ring-1 ring-offset-1 ring-offset-background ring-border font-mono after:hidden before:hidden inline-flex items-center justify-center gap-2 rounded text-xs font-semibold text-foreground px-1">auto</code> means the model can pick between generating a message or calling one or more tools. <code class="relative mx-1 bg-background !text-[0.765em] ring-1 ring-offset-1 ring-offset-background ring-border font-mono after:hidden before:hidden inline-flex items-center justify-center gap-2 rounded text-xs font-semibold text-foreground px-1">any</code> or <code class="relative mx-1 bg-background !text-[0.765em] ring-1 ring-offset-1 ring-offset-background ring-border font-mono after:hidden before:hidden inline-flex items-center justify-center gap-2 rounded text-xs font-semibold text-foreground px-1">required</code> means the model must call one or more tools. Specifying a particular tool via <code class="relative mx-1 bg-background !text-[0.765em] ring-1 ring-offset-1 ring-offset-background ring-border font-mono after:hidden before:hidden inline-flex items-center justify-center gap-2 rounded text-xs font-semibold text-foreground px-1">\{&quot;type&quot;: &quot;function&quot;, &quot;function&quot;: \{&quot;name&quot;: &quot;my_function&quot;\}\}</code> forces the model to call that tool.</p></article></div></div></div></div></div></div></div></div><div data-slot="connecting-cell" data-bottom-connection="none" data-top-connection="none" data-right-connection="none" class="group flex flex-row group/connecting-cell"><div class="flex-1 h-full max-w-full" data-type="connecting-cell-content"><div slot="entry" slot="description"><p class="text-secondary-foreground/93">Nucleus sampling, where the model considers the results of the tokens with <code class="relative mx-1 bg-background !text-[0.765em] ring-1 ring-offset-1 ring-offset-background ring-border font-mono after:hidden before:hidden inline-flex items-center justify-center gap-2 rounded text-xs font-semibold text-foreground px-1">top_p</code> probability mass. So 0.1 means only the tokens comprising the top 10% probability mass are considered. We generally recommend altering this or <code class="relative mx-1 bg-background !text-[0.765em] ring-1 ring-offset-1 ring-offset-background ring-border font-mono after:hidden before:hidden inline-flex items-center justify-center gap-2 rounded text-xs font-semibold text-foreground px-1">temperature</code> but not
